entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer with multi-level retention across users, sessions, and agent states. It utilizes temporal reasoning and hybrid retrieval to ensure agents maintain a time-aware understanding of both past and future events.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated memory system features a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without requiring expensive LLM calls. It enhances context through a hybrid search mechanism that combines standard vector retrieval with backlink-boosted graph ranking.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'learn-as-you-go' architecture focused on long-term retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It provides enterprise-grade storage options via PostgreSQL and Oracle, allowing agents to evolve their knowledge base through a simple two-line LLM wrapper.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions from message traces and event logs. Its peer-centric model is designed to track evolving entities and multi-peer perspectives using a combination of BM25 and vector search.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive harness uses five specialized agent roles to create an iterative self-improvement loop for complex tasks. It persists knowledge by generating structured playbooks and detailed execution traces specifically optimized for Hermes and Claude agents.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides lossless context management by organizing conversation history into a hierarchical DAG structure. This allows for depth-aware compaction while ensuring raw history remains accessible via SQLite-backed tools for grepping and expanding context.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent users. It includes an automated setup script for Docker and Redis, supporting any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP to provide hybrid retrieval across vector stores, BM25, and knowledge graphs for privacy-conscious agent deployments.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference efficiency, ContextPilot reduces prompt tokens by up to 36% through aggressive context deduplication. It significantly boosts prefill speeds for Hermes Agent and vLLM by optimizing prefix sharing and context optimization.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format to facilitate zero-cost local learning. It employs ACT-R cognitive principles to strengthen relevant facts over time while allowing stale information to naturally decay.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep acts as a reflective memory system that indexes code, documents, and media using OCR and automatic summarization. It builds navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes via edge tags, making it a robust knowledge base for agent tool integration.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory plugin for Hermes that prioritizes speed, offering sub-millisecond latency for all search and storage operations. It leverages SQLite with native vector support to provide a lightweight, local-first memory solution.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It utilizes a local-first stack with SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG via an MCP-native server.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This project provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It uses the agentskills.io format to enable sub-second discovery and execution across 26 specialized security domains.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw automates the deduplication and quality improvement of skills across different agent frameworks and devices. It allows user groups to share learned skills, enabling a collective evolution of agent capabilities over time.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It utilizes a two-pass audit system and a comprehensive word replacement table to ensure consistent, human-like output.
entries.wondelai/skills: This is a modular, cross-platform library compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io agents. It offers a marketplace of skills covering diverse professional fields including architecture, UX design, and product strategy.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This implementation of the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI uses progressive disclosure to reduce token usage. It supports both programmatic Python definitions and filesystem-based skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, exporting them to formats like XML, SVG, or PDF. It includes specialized presets for UML and architecture diagrams alongside an iterative self-check refinement loop.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It generates constraint reports to highlight analysis blind spots and limitations during problem-solving.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes CLI tools for local simulations and monitoring cross-chain smart contract workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by weaving runnable source code and prose into unified .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract code for execution and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus facilitates cross-instance shared memory and the automated extraction of training data from agent workflows. It is designed to help agents 'train their replacement' through a pipeline of decision-quality tagging and model fine-tuning.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock.io is a registry for reusable AI skills that features recursive dependency resolution and an OpenAPI-driven SDK. It uniquely integrates a commerce flow supporting both Stripe and the TON blockchain for skill transactions.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation models. They support precise technical controls such as hex color specification and JSON structured prompting.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both the necessary Python scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation to quickly expand an agent's toolkit.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running agents that manages state through JSON snapshots and structured mission lifecycles. It facilitates complex tasks using a plan-approve-execute flow and generates markdown briefs for session handoffs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and real-time progress tracking. It ensures operational safety by providing rollback procedures for recovery if a task fails.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token consumption when handling large toolsets. It utilizes a socket-based daemon to maintain persistent connections across multiple Model Context Protocol servers.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with line-level citations for all generated skills.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust scores for different skill creators.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and automatic registration of skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill allows Hermes to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts using standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It features a guided setup script for secure configuration via Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for AI coding agents, allowing users to execute Hermes directly via nix run without manual installation. It is ideal for developers who want the latest versions of Hermes and other major coding tools within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers. It features dynamic policy presets to strictly control network and filesystem access while supporting over 40 built-in skills for automated workflows.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix integration. It is the premier choice for users seeking a fully reproducible, system-level deployment that supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI and 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It integrates local model support via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for building custom automation pipelines on a local machine.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted home directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using simple build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: Serving as a Docker-first control plane, this application orchestrates multiple Hermes instances with separate workspaces and unique identities. It facilitates enterprise-grade operations through hierarchy-aware agent delegation, task scheduling, and MCP access for external AI clients.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: This project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for production-ready autonomous execution, specifically supporting recurring AI cron jobs and Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal interface for easy browser-based access. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions and GHCR for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: Mission Control is a self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows from frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require local task dispatching, spend monitoring, and a human-in-the-loop review system for final task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes via Ollama and OpenRouter. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that allows swarms to evolve their capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A specialized meta-optimization tool, this project uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers by mutating and comparing candidate agent configurations.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and live scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a massive fleet of 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to handle complex software engineering pipelines. It enforces strict architectural quality gates and security audits, ensuring that every code change passes through mandatory research, implementation, and QA phases.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes Agent with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase development process, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity from initial planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation framework routes tasks between Hermes subagents and external models like Codex or Claude Code while preventing context pollution. It focuses on operational safety by enforcing isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local data visualization for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding environments. It is essential for Hermes developers who need to monitor resource usage alongside other agents like Claude Code and Gemini.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files. It supports custom ASCII banners and spinner animations, offering a way to create and share unique visual themes while inheriting default system behaviors.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for a native, process-free experience. It eliminates the need for OAuth and external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool exposes web navigation to agents via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a supervisory UI with annotated checkpoints, allowing developers to audit and supervise Hermes Agent as it performs browser-based tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It allows for seamless switching between default and local agents, each with its own project-specific memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that performs deterministic scans of agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It helps developers maintain high-quality Hermes implementations by detecting seven specific structural failure modes, including H1-H7 patterns.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth onboarding process by handling the transfer of complex model configurations and local credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, enabling a zero-cost agent environment in minutes.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build Python and JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-friendly web environment. It features a streamlined three-panel layout that organizes chat, file browsing, and session management into a single cohesive view.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: This dashboard serves as a multi-platform command center, offering unified configuration for eight different channels including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. It distinguishes itself with real-time usage analytics, token tracking, and an integrated web terminal for remote backend management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: Designed as a native web workspace, this project features a 'Swarm Mode' for the role-based dispatch of multiple persistent agents. It integrates chat, memory browsing, and terminal operations into a unified dashboard that works with vanilla Hermes installations without requiring forks.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. It includes a split-view file editor with syntax highlighting and detailed performance metrics to track model costs and token usage in real-time.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS application provides a direct SSH connection to Hermes sessions without requiring a middle-tier API or daemon. It offers a unique productivity view that combines remote terminal access with Kanban boards, cron job management, and file browsing.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard is built specifically as a Telegram Mini App, featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It allows users to spawn up to five concurrent background agents while monitoring real-time system metrics like CPU and memory usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows GUI provides a portable environment for Hermes with granular permission controls and over 100 integrated tools, including TTS and ComfyUI. It stands out with a dynamic tool maker that allows users to create custom API wrappers at runtime without administrative rights.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process with guided installation and dependency resolution for both local and remote backends. It supports 14 distinct toolsets, including vision and code execution, while managing gateway connections for various messaging platforms.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on process monitoring and health, this dashboard provides real-time tracking of session activity, service status, and operational costs. It features full-text search for inspecting conversation histories and a dedicated interface for browsing skill sources and scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This chat-first workspace utilizes SSE streaming and tool approval cards to give users fine-grained control over agent actions. It includes an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Built on a single-file React architecture, this interface features a glassmorphic design with real-time log streaming and inline workspace previews. It provides a lightweight web-based management layer for handling MCP tools, skills, and automated agent tasks.
